mappings.rationale: Directly covers structural systems, foundations, construction techniques, and the engineering of buildings and civil works.
Directly covers civil engineering works including dams, bridges, tunnels, and harbour construction.
Covers the broad engineering principles underlying general building construction and civil engineering works.
Directly relevant to building design, construction planning, and the relationship between architecture and construction technology.
Covers modern building design and construction methods including reinforced concrete and steel construction.
Relevant to historical construction techniques and building components across architectural periods.
Relevant to preconstruction planning, site design, and the integration of buildings with surrounding landscapes.
Covers building materials including concrete, steel, glass, plastics, and their properties and testing.
Relevant to preconstruction planning, design programming, and the integration of buildings into urban infrastructure.
Relevant to surveying procedures and techniques for laying out building foundations.
Relevant to building materials sourced from earth including stone, sand, clay, and gravel.
Relevant to stone as a building material and the geological properties affecting construction.
Relevant to acoustics and sound-control techniques in building construction.
